revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24618454543|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Aoi-jinja]][[ja:青渭神社]] {{Expand Japanese|青渭神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Aoi Shrine | native_name = 青渭神社 | image = Aoi Shinto Shrine in Inagi in 2009.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Aoi Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35.642808|139.501245}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|az|Sarutahiko|en|Sarutahiko Ōkami|fa|ساروتاهیکو اوکامی|fr|Sarutahiko Ōkami|it|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|サルタヒコ|ko|사루타히코 오카미|mni|ꯁꯔꯨꯇꯍꯤꯀꯣ ꯑꯣꯀꯃꯤ|pl|Sarutahiko|ru|Сарутахико|simple|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ta|சருதாகிகோ அகாமி|tr|Sarutahiko Ōkami|zh|猿田彥|qid=Q3090037|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} }} {{nihongo|'''Aoi Shrine'''|青渭神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Q134917287|ltq=Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} * {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|lt=Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国の式内社一覧|qid=Q11546334|1=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province}} ** Series ordinal: 11 ** P155: {{ill|Q135039730|ltq=Q135039730|qid=Q135039730|1=Torakashiwa Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q135039732|ltq=Q135039732|qid=Q135039732|1=Atachino Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Tama district|lt=Tama district|ja|多摩郡|ko|다마군|zh|多摩郡|qid=Q11430789|1=Tama district}} * {{ill|Q907398|ltq=Q907398|qid=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|35.642808|139.501245}} == image (P18) == * Aoi Shinto Shrine in Inagi in 2009.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Q3090037|ltq=Q3090037|qid=Q3090037|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} * {{ill|Q462803|ltq=Q462803|qid=Q462803|1=Ame-no-Uzume}} == described by source (P1343) == * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|simple|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|zh|延喜式神名帳|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181699 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917316|ltq=Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه شینتو|fr|Directive shinto|id|Pengarahan Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * -ヌマノ == Commons category (P373) == * Aoi-jinja == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Q130307202|ltq=Q130307202|qid=Q130307202|1=Aoi Shrine}} * {{ill|Aoi Shrine|lt=Aoi Shrine|ja|青渭神社 (調布市)|qid=Q130307205|1=Aoi Shrine}} * {{ill|Q130307206|ltq=Q130307206|qid=Q130307206|1=Aoi Shrine}} * {{ill|Q135039731|ltq=Q135039731|qid=Q135039731|1=Awowino Shrine}} ** P2868: {{ill|Shikinai Ronsha|lt=Shikinai Ronsha|ja|論社|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '東京都稲城市東長沼1053', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '東京都調布市深大寺', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Aoi-jinja}} [[JA:青渭神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11662605}} == Japanese content == {{Source citation needed|date=July 26, 2015 (Sun) 07:00 (UTC)}} '''Aoi Shrine''' (あおいじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in the former {{ill|lt=Tama District|Q11430789|qid=Q11430789|1=Tama district}} of {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|qid=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|Q1490|qid=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}. It is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as “Aoi Shrine, Tama District, Musashi Province,” but there are three shrines regarded as candidate sites ({{ill|lt=ronsha|Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}) of the Engishiki Aoi Shrine: those in {{ill|lt=Higashinaganuma, Inagi City|Q30923484|qid=Q30923484|1=Higashinaganuma, Inagi City}}, {{ill|lt=Jindaiji Motomachi, Chōfu City|Q97172837|qid=Q97172837|1=Jindaiji Motomachi}}, and {{ill|lt=Sawai, Ōme City|Q112152778|qid=Q112152778|lt=Sawai|1=Sawai}}. === Aoi Shrine in Inagi City === {{Shrine |Name = Aoi Shrine
(Inagi City, Tokyo) |Image = [[File:Aoi Shinto Shrine in Inagi in 2009.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (April 2009)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|38|33.7|N|139|30|4.2|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|Q1490|qid=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Inagi City|Q317847|qid=Q317847|1=Inagi}}{{ill|lt=Higashinaganuma|Q30923484|qid=Q30923484|1=Higashinaganuma}} 1053 |Coordinates = {{coord|35|38|33.7|N|139|30|4.2|E|display=inline}} |Enshrined deities = Aoi-no-Kami, {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|qid=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Ōkami|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|qid=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Uzume}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}; former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Established = Unknown{{sfn|Tokyo Association of Shinto Shrines, Inagi City}} (traditionally during the {{ill|lt=Kōnin|Q1373070|qid=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} era) |Main hall = |Other names = |Annual festival = First Sunday of October{{sfn|Tokyo Association of Shinto Shrines, Inagi City}} |Rituals = }} It is located at 1053 Higashinaganuma, Inagi City, Tokyo{{sfn|Tokyo Association of Shinto Shrines, Inagi City}}, about a five-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Inagi-Naganuma Station|Q5366278|qid=Q5366278|1=Inagi-Naganuma Station}} on the JR {{ill|lt=Nambu Line|Q1366263|qid=Q1366263|1=Nambu Line}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. The enshrined deities are Aoi-no-Kami, {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|qid=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}}, and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|qid=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Uzume}}{{sfn|Tokyo Association of Shinto Shrines, Inagi City}}. As suggested by the place name “Naganuma,” this area was a {{ill|lt=floodplain|Q193110|qid=Q193110|1=Floodplain}} of the {{ill|lt=Tama River|Q1063006|qid=Q1063006|1=Tama River}} and had long been marshland. Accordingly, the shrine was formerly also called Ōnuma Myōjin or Aonuma Daimyōjin. Both this Naganuma area and Jindaiji are places closely associated with water, and Aoi-no-Kami is considered to be a {{ill|lt=water deity|Q3137449|qid=Q3137449|1=Suijin}}. The date of establishment is unknown, but tradition holds that it was founded during the {{ill|lt=Kōnin|Q1373070|qid=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} era (810–824). The current {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine buildings|1=UNKNOWN}} are reinforced {{ill|lt=concrete|Q22657|qid=Q22657|1=Concrete}} structures built in {{ill|lt=1974|Q2478|qid=Q2478|1=1974}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 49), while the main sanctuary housed within the protective hall is believed to have been constructed in the early {{ill|lt=17th century|Q7016|qid=Q7016|1=17th century}}. There are two pairs (four in total) of {{ill|lt=komainu|Q20155846|qid=Q20155846|1=Komainu}}. On the reverse side of the pair closer to the main sanctuary (shown in the photo on the right) is an inscription reading “{{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|qid=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} 13” ({{ill|lt=1830|Q7576|qid=Q7576|1=1830}}). The other pair bears an inscription from the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} period. The shrine grounds are long and narrow from north to south. Although nearby spec houses using modern building materials detract from the atmosphere, venerable old trees remain, conveying the sense of an ancient sacred precinct. According to elderly locals who remember the past, countless large trees once grew densely here, making it dark even at midday. At the grand festival, a {{ill|lt=lion dance|Q713866|qid=Q713866|1=Lion dance}} known as the “Aoi Shishimai” is dedicated. {{clear}} === Aoi Shrine in Chōfu City === {{Shrine |Name = Aoi Shrine
(Chōfu City, Tokyo) |Image = [[File:Aoi-jinja in Chofu 01 - Jan 1, 2019.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (January 2019)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|40|2|N|139|33|10.8|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|Q1490|qid=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Chōfu City|Q210667|qid=Q210667|1=Chōfu}}{{ill|lt=Jindaiji Motomachi|Q97172837|qid=Q97172837|1=Jindaiji Motomachi}} 5-17-10 |Coordinates = {{coord|35|40|2|N|139|33|10.8|E|display=inline}} |Enshrined deity = Aoi Ōkami{{sfn|Tokyo Association of Shinto Shrines, Chōfu City}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Aonuma Manuma Oshihime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}) |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}; former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Established = Unknown{{sfn|Tokyo Association of Shinto Shrines, Chōfu City}} |Annual festival = Second Sunday of October }} {{Main|The Zelkova of Aoi Shrine}} It is located at 5-17-10 Jindaiji Motomachi, Chōfu City, Tokyo{{sfn|Tokyo Association of Shinto Shrines, Chōfu City}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}{{sfn|Tokyo Association of Shinto Shrines, Chōfu City}}. Although the date of establishment is unknown, it has long been revered as the chief {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=tutelary shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} of the Jindaiji area. In the past, it possessed shrine lands exceeding five chō. Within the grounds there was a large pond fed by spring water, which shimmered with blue waves; hence it was also called Aonami Tenjin Shrine. It has deep ties with {{ill|lt=Jindaiji Temple|Q500736|qid=Q500736|1=Jindai-ji (Tokyo)}}. The enshrined deity, Aoi Ōkami, is identified with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Aonuma Manuma Oshihime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, and it is also said that the shrine worshiped a great serpent dwelling in the large pond. In any case, it may safely be regarded as a water deity. The {{ill|lt=sacred tree|Q48743901|qid=Q48743901|1=Shinboku}}, the {{ill|lt=great zelkova of Aoi Shrine|Q24868795|qid=Q24868795|1=great zelkova of Aoi Shrine}}, is a giant tree recorded in works such as the {{ill|lt=Edo Meisho Zue|Q2704637|qid=Q2704637|1=Edo meisho zue}} and is designated a {{ill|lt=Natural Monument|Q23790|qid=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} of the city. {{clear}} === Aoi Shrine in Ōme City === {{Shrine |Name=Aoi Shrine
(Ōme City, Tokyo) |Image=[[File:Sougakusan jinja.JPG|250px]]
Okumiya at the summit of Mount Sōgaku
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|48|32.3|N|139|11|1.6|E}}}} |Location={{ill|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|Q1490|qid=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Ōme City|Q237683|qid=Q237683|1=Ōme, Tokyo}} Sawai 3-chōme 1060 {{sfn|Tokyo Association of Shinto Shrines, Ōme City}} |Coordinates = {{coord|35|48|32.3|N|139|11|1.6|E|display=inline}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}{{sfn|Tokyo Association of Shinto Shrines, Ōme City}} |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}{{sfn|Tokyo Association of Shinto Shrines, Ōme City}}; former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Established=Unknown |Annual festival=April 18 (or the nearest Sunday){{sfn|Tokyo Association of Shinto Shrines, Ōme City}} }} [[File:Aoi jinja izumi.JPG|thumb|left|200px|Aoi no I, near the summit of Mount Sōgaku]] The shrine has its inner sanctuary (okumiya) at the summit of {{ill|lt=Mount Sōgaku|Q11492964|qid=Q11492964|1=Mount Sōgaku}} in Ōme City, Tokyo (Sawai 3-chōme 1060), and its village shrine (satomiya) at Sawai 3-chōme 639. Its former shrine rank was Gōsha. The principal enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}. Near the summit of Mount Sōgaku is a sacred spring known as Mana-i{{sfn|Tokyo Association of Shinto Shrines, Ōme City}}. It is also called Aoi no I (“Well of Aoi”) and is said to be the origin of the shrine’s name. The date of establishment is unknown, but according to shrine tradition, in the 7th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|qid=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}, when an epidemic spread throughout the country, the {{ill|lt=emperor|Q208233|qid=Q208233|1=Emperor of Japan}} prayed to the deities in various regions and donated sacred land to this shrine, where rites were performed. It is said that during the {{ill|lt=Tengyō|Q1327340|qid=Q1327340|1=Tengyō}} era (938–947), {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Tsunemoto|Q3099236|qid=Q3099236|1=Minamoto no Tsunemoto}} constructed shrine buildings. The present shrine buildings were rebuilt in the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Kōka|Q1325317|qid=Q1325317|1=Kōka}}. In the early {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} period, a worship hall was built at the foot of Mount Sōgaku and designated as a distant worship hall{{sfn|Tokyo Association of Shinto Shrines, Ōme City}}. Today, the main shrine at the summit is treated as the inner sanctuary, while the distant worship hall at the mountain’s base serves as the village shrine, where festivals and rites are conducted. {{clear}} === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/inagi/5571/|title=Inagi City – Aoi Shrine|website=Tokyo Association of Shinto Shrines|accessdate=2020-7-8|ref={{sfnref|Tokyo Association of Shinto Shrines, Inagi City}}}} * {{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/kitatama/chofu/5532/|title=Chōfu City – Aoi Shrine|website=Tokyo Association of Shinto Shrines|accessdate=2020-7-8|ref={{sfnref|Tokyo Association of Shinto Shrines, Chōfu City}}}} * {{Cite web|Japanese|url= http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/nishitama/ome/3168/|title=Ōme City – Aoi Shrine [Osōgaku-sama]|website=Tokyo Association of Shinto Shrines|accessdate=2020-7-8|ref={{sfnref|Tokyo Association of Shinto Shrines, Ōme City}}}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|Japanese|title={{ill|lt=Newly Compiled Gazetteer of Musashi Province|Q11503943|qid=Q11503943|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}}|volume=Vol. 94, Tama District No. 6|chapter=Jindaiji Village – Aonami Tenjin Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763989/25}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6}} {{Quotation|“This is said to be the Aoi Shrine listed in the Engishiki.”}} * {{cite book|Japanese|title=Newly Compiled Gazetteer of Musashi Province|volume=Vol. 95, Tama District No. 7|chapter=Naganuma Village – Aonuma Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763989/46}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6}} {{Quotation|“It is thought that the Aoi Shrine listed in the Jinmyōchō may well be this shrine.”}} * {{cite book|Japanese|title=Newly Compiled Gazetteer of Musashi Province|volume=Vol. 116, Tama District No. 28|chapter=Sawai Village – Aoi Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763993/101}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6}} {{Quotation|“This shrine is one that is recorded in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō.”}} === External links === [https://aoi-jinja.com/ Aoi Shrine | General Shrine of Jindaiji, Chōfu] – Official website {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Aoi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Aoi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] Tokyo]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Inagi, Tokyo]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chōfu, Tokyo]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ōme, Tokyo]] [[Category:incorrect template category applied]]
